name: Pituffik Space Base
tagline: The U.S. military's northernmost installation, a remote Arctic missile-warning and space-surveillance base in Greenland.
_note: True OCONUS (Greenland), REMOTE/UNACCOMPANIED Arctic tour. Renamed from Thule Air Base to Pituffik Space Base (April 2023); now a Space Force installation (missile warning/BMEWS + space surveillance + satellite control). Northernmost US base. Families do NOT relocate here -> built on the unaccompanied/remote pattern: no family housing/OHA/DoDEA/BAH/RentCast/SchoolDigger/rent-buy. 6th Space Force base in app -> Space Force chip. Personnel live in on-base quarters; extreme cold/dark winters; access by military air only. Facts verified from official sources (spaceforce.mil/Pituffik, Wikipedia for corroborated headline facts). Medium-confidence flags: base coords (approx); exact org/delta designation.

data.Location: Pituffik, northwest Greenland (above the Arctic Circle)
data.Mission: Missile warning, space surveillance, and satellite control
data.Name note: Renamed from Thule Air Base to Pituffik Space Base in 2023

sections.title: Mission & Identity
data: Pituffik Space Base (formerly Thule Air Base) is the U.S. military's northernmost installation, deep above the Arctic Circle in Greenland. It performs missile warning, space surveillance, and satellite control, watching the northern approaches to North America.
It's one of the most remote and extreme assignments in the U.S. military: isolated, with months of polar darkness and brutal cold, reached only by military aircraft.

data.subtitle: This is not a family-relocation assignment. Pituffik is a remote, unaccompanied tour where personnel live in on-base quarters, so the planning is about the separation and the extreme environment, not housing or schools.
intro: Because of the extreme isolation and Arctic conditions, Pituffik is an unaccompanied tour, typically one year. Dependents do not relocate here, so there's no family housing, OHA, BAH, or DoDEA school in the usual sense.
The real planning is for the family back home, the separation, and adapting to months of polar night, extreme cold, and a small, tight-knit base community.

recommendations.advice: Personnel live in on-base housing/dormitory quarters; there's no off-base community in this remote part of Greenland.
Since it's unaccompanied, sort out where your family will live during the tour and your finances and communication plan early.
Months of darkness, extreme cold, and isolation are the defining challenge; lean on the base community and morale programs.
Remote/unaccompanied tours have their own pay and allowance rules; verify everything (including any continued BAH for family back home) with finance.

sections.title: Access
data: Pituffik is an extremely remote, access-controlled installation reached only by military air. Travel is tightly coordinated through your command.
Requirements are specific and change with conditions, so coordinate everything through your command before you travel.

sections.title: Housing (On-Base Quarters)
data: Because this is a remote, unaccompanied tour, personnel live in on-base quarters; there is no family housing or off-base community in this isolated part of Greenland.
Your unit handles your quarters assignment on arrival; confirm current arrangements through your sponsor and the base.

sections.title: Schools
data: Because Pituffik is a remote, unaccompanied tour, dependents do not relocate here, and there is no DoDEA school.
If you have school-age children, they remain at your designated location during the tour; coordinate schooling there and confirm your specific situation with your command.

data.Care: Basic primary and urgent care for the assigned population; anything beyond requires medical evacuation off-island given the remoteness

data.Exchange: On-base exchange (BX) for the assigned population
data.Note: Shopping is limited to what's on base; there's no local town to shop in

intro: A remote, unaccompanied Arctic tour has its own checklist. The focus is on-base in-processing and squaring away your family's situation back home, not setting up a household here.

steps.title: Coordinate travel with your command
steps.text: Travel to Pituffik is by military air and tightly coordinated; confirm your flight, gear, and entitlements well in advance.

steps.title: In-process and update DEERS
steps.text: Report to your unit and finance, update DEERS, and get the required ID and logistics cards.

steps.title: Get your quarters set up
steps.text: Coordinate your on-base quarters with your unit; this is your home for the tour.

steps.title: Square away the home front
steps.text: Confirm where your family lives during the tour, sort finances, allotments, powers of attorney, and a communication plan for the year apart.

steps.title: Prepare for the Arctic
steps.text: Get issued/learn the extreme cold-weather gear and routines; understand the polar-night schedule and morale resources.

steps.title: Set up healthcare
steps.text: Enroll in the TRICARE Overseas Program; know that anything beyond basic care means medical evacuation off-island.

data.q: Can my family come to Pituffik?
data.a: No. It's a remote, unaccompanied tour (typically one year) in extreme Arctic conditions, so dependents don't relocate here, and there's no family housing or DoDEA school.
Pituffik Space Base, as of 2023. It was Thule Air Base, and became a Space Force installation under its Greenlandic name.

data.q: Do I get BAH or OHA?
data.a: This is an unaccompanied remote tour, so the usual family housing allowances don't apply the same way. Confirm your specific pay and allowances (and any continued BAH for family back home) with finance.
Extreme: months of polar darkness, brutal cold, and deep isolation, with a small, tight-knit base community and strong morale programs.

sections.title: On-Base & Around
sections.note: Pituffik is extremely remote; there is no local town. These are the realities of the area, not destinations to visit.

picks.name: Arctic landscape
picks.blurb: Glaciers, sea ice, and tundra surround the base; the northern lights are a highlight.

picks.name: Base MWR / community
picks.blurb: On-base recreation and community programs anchor life during the tour.

picks.name: North Star Bay
picks.blurb: The bay and Mount Dundas near the base, frozen much of the year.

picks.name: Polar night & midnight sun
picks.blurb: Months of continuous darkness in winter and continuous daylight in summer.
